architecture.summary: The system uses large language models to generate sequential Socratic subquestions that guide math word problem solving. Questions are generated via input conditioning and reinforcement learning schemes, then used either to prompt the LM solver directly (text-in-context) or to guide human solvers. The LM reads the problem statement and prior questions/answers, generates the next question constrained by learned properties, and the solver (human or model) processes it to advance toward the final answer.
trained_components: reward model for question quality constraints

biblio.title: Automatic Generation of Socratic Subquestions for Teaching Math Word Problems

claims.statement: LMs constrained with desirable question properties generate superior questions and improve the overall performance of a math word problem solver
Socratic questioning strategy can enhance both human performance and assist math word problem solvers
The difficulty level of problems plays an important role in determining whether questioning improves or hinders human performance
Large language models show ability in generating sequential questions for guiding math word problem-solving
Both automatic and human quality evaluations confirm that constrained question generation outperforms unconstrained approaches
